dimensions.name: reliability
dimensions.why: Delivery guarantees, retries, and failure handling decide whether jobs survive crashes.

dimensions.name: throughput
dimensions.why: Sustained jobs/sec and latency under load determine fit for high-volume workloads.

dimensions.name: operational complexity
dimensions.why: Backing store, deployment, and observability burden drive total cost of ownership.

rationale: Five widely-used job/queue systems already documented in the catalog, compared on the three dimensions that most distinguish them: reliability, throughput, and operational complexity.
